body_txt: Despair has its advantages. Your expectations are gone, so you can't be disappointed. Your feelings are dead, so you can't feel any pain, and you have nothing to lose, so you don't need to worry about your future. You can find peace there, because you have passed safely out of reach of anything that could trouble you , but if, in that state, someone inspires you with even a fragment of hope , all of your peace is gone. Now that you've started to feel again, you can feel pain again. Now that you have someone or something that you care about, you're afraid of losing them. And now that you've let yourself hope again, you risk being disappointed again . You used to take comfort from knowing that the worst was over, but now you are afraid that the worst is yet to come... and of course, if you believe things can't get better, then you feel no obligation to try; but if they can get better... you may suddenly feel a ton of daunting reponsibility as you conclude there are difficult or dangerous things you can and must do. Then again, the hope can be even scarier if it's of a sort that you have no control over at all, and it's in the hands of others to make it happen. Characters who feel this mood (usually) haven't actually crossed the Despair Event Horizon : they have perhaps just hit a Heroic BSoD or worn stronger-than-usual Jade-Colored Glasses . This moment, when they start to feel hope again, and try to reject it, is usually just a blip on the road to recovery from the despairing state. Common for the Broken Bird to feel it. Compare Safety in Indifference , the emotional state of most characters who feel that Hope is Scary, and Love Hurts .
